lessons.subject: Mathematics
lessons.title: Example - Comparing Fractions with Visual Models
lessons.objectives: Example objective: compare simple fractions using visual representations and manipulatives.
lessons.indicators: Needs chunked instructions and repeated modeling
lessons.support_types: Visual aids, guided practice, peer support
lessons.custom_support: Example support: provide sentence starters and oral response options.

lessons.subject: Science
lessons.title: Example - Ecosystems and Food Webs
lessons.objectives: Example objective: describe relationships in a local ecosystem using a class-made food web.
lessons.indicators: Needs short tasks with visual prompts
lessons.support_types: Graphic organizer, movement breaks, paired discussion
lessons.custom_support: Example support: use color-coded cards for producers and consumers.

reflections.subject: Example - Mathematics
reflections.lesson_plan_linked: Example - Comparing Fractions with Visual Models
reflections.strategies_used: Used concrete fraction strips and think-pair-share.
reflections.learner_response: Most learners engaged better after visual scaffolds were introduced.
reflections.worked_well: Guided examples and peer support increased participation.
reflections.needs_improvement: Need clearer pacing for independent practice.
reflections.challenges: A few learners needed repeated instructions.
reflections.next_steps: Prepare shorter task chunks and add recap prompts.

reflections.subject: Example - Science
reflections.strategies_used: Used station rotation and visual anchor charts.
reflections.learner_response: Learners collaborated well in small groups.
reflections.next_steps: Add timed cues and assign transition roles.

observations.subject: Example - Mathematics, Grade 5
observations.notes: Learners benefited from visual cues and flexible grouping.

observations.subject: Example - Science, Grade 6
observations.notes: Clear routines and transitions improved class flow.

pre: I can plan lessons that accommodate learners with diverse needs.
I can modify lesson objectives appropriately for learners with SEN.
I feel confident selecting inclusive support strategies.
I can contextualize lessons to meet the needs of my diverse learners.
I know how to document reflections after teaching an inclusive lesson.
